callRationale: Started with a broad official-release/changelog search for TypeScript or JavaScript releases after August 2025 with API changes.
Broadened from TypeScript 6.0 to library/framework candidates because Next.js 16 and React 19.2 were stronger matches for a JavaScript/TypeScript library release.
Fetched the official Next.js 16 announcement and upgrade guide to verify the release date, post-August-2025 freshness, and migration-level API changes.
Fetched official Next.js API reference pages for use cache, revalidateTag, updateTag, and proxy to obtain concrete call signatures and code examples.
strengths: Final answer is grounded in official Next.js release notes, upgrade docs, and API reference pages.
Evidence includes a release date after August 2025 and more than three concrete API changes with code-shaped examples.
Provider returned useful excerpts with API signatures and migration examples, especially for caching and proxy APIs.
weaknesses: The first search was broad and returned TypeScript/compiler results plus some non-official or less relevant pages before narrowing to Next.js.
The provider schema lacks explicit source-policy controls, so official-source preference had to be encoded in the objective text.
notableFailures: The Next.js 16 blog result did not expose a normal publish_date field, but the page excerpt included official frontmatter with publishedAt: October 21st 2025.
